raw_text: {"round_id":"CB-2026-06-12-1W","model_id":"anthropic-claude-opus-4-8","provider":"anthropic","mode":"closed_capability","portfolio":[{"option_id":"CONSUMER_STAPLES","allocation_pct":30,"rationale":"Defensive sector with strong recent momentum (+3.94% 7d), benefits during recent equity selloff and FOMC week uncertainty."},{"option_id":"LOW_VOL","allocation_pct":25,"rationale":"Low-vol factor showing relative strength (+1.96% 7d) into a high-event week with FOMC; defensive ballast."},{"option_id":"HEALTHCARE","allocation_pct":20,"rationale":"Defensive sector with positive momentum (+1.32% 7d, +5.65% 30d), low policy-event risk in window."},{"option_id":"AEROSPACE_DEFENSE","allocation_pct":15,"rationale":"Strong relative momentum (+1.93% 7d) with geopolitical/defense budget tailwinds; outperforming broad market."},{"option_id":"SHORT_TREASURY","allocation_pct":10,"rationale":"Cash-like stability through FOMC decision; minimizes drawdown risk while earning carry."}],"confidence":0.55,"portfolio_rationale":"With sticky inflation (CPI YoY 4.2%, PPI 6.5%), a likely FOMC hold June 17, elevated valuations (forward P/E 21.1), and a recent broad equity selloff, I tilt defensive toward staples, low-vol, healthcare and defense which showed relative strength, aiming to outperform a vulnerable cap-weighted S&P 500."},"rationale_summary":"Defensive sector and factor tilt expected to outperform a stretched, rate-sensitive S&P 500 through a high-event FOMC week with sticky inflation.","key_risks":["A dovish FOMC surprise on June 17 could spark a growth/tech rally, causing defensive sectors to lag the benchmark.","Strong May retail sales (June 17) could reignite cyclical/discretionary leadership over defensives.","Healthcare carries idiosyncratic policy headline risk despite no scheduled events.","Defense names can reverse sharply on any geopolitical de-escalation headlines."]}
